Deterministic vs. Probabilistic

Deterministic means the same input always produces the same output. No variation, no surprises.

Think of a vending machine: press B4, you always get the same snack. It never decides to give you something else because it “felt like it.”

Probabilistic means the output is guessed based on patterns. The same input can produce different results.

AI chatbots and LLMs are probabilistic. Ask the same question twice and you may get two different answers. They predict what sounds right based on patterns they’ve seen - which makes them flexible, but they can also produce errors or “hallucinations” where they confidently generate something incorrect.

Why this matters

Deterministic systems are predictable and auditable. If something appears, you can trace exactly why.

Probabilistic systems are flexible but unreliable for documentation - you can’t trust that the same request will yield the same accurate result every time.
